testcase.name: idempotency decorators and metrics > attaches idempotency metadata with default and custom options
idempotency decorators and metrics > tracks replay counts in memory
IdempotencyInterceptor > rejects annotated routes without the configured header
IdempotencyInterceptor > uses the default header name and rejects blank header values
IdempotencyInterceptor > bypasses non-annotated routes
IdempotencyInterceptor > bypasses routes explicitly marked as non-idempotent
IdempotencyInterceptor > replays cached responses for the same key and payload
IdempotencyInterceptor > replays backend entries with the default status code
IdempotencyInterceptor > accepts canonical lower-case array headers and user principal fallbacks
IdempotencyInterceptor > continues without durable ownership when no backend is configured and strict mode is off
IdempotencyInterceptor > falls through pending backend and durable entries before executing the request
IdempotencyInterceptor > returns 422 when the same key is reused with a different body
IdempotencyInterceptor > uses the durable store when configured
IdempotencyInterceptor > does not persist 5xx responses and allows retry
IdempotencyInterceptor > throws service unavailable when lock cannot be obtained in strict mode
IdempotencyInterceptor > waits for a completed backend entry when lock ownership is already held
IdempotencyInterceptor > records metrics when waiting returns a completed backend entry
IdempotencyInterceptor > waits for a completed durable entry and applies default replay headers
IdempotencyInterceptor > records metrics when replaying a completed durable entry
IdempotencyInterceptor > continues waiting over pending durable entries until a completed one is ready
IdempotencyInterceptor > continues past pending backend entries while waiting for ownership
IdempotencyInterceptor > executes the request after wait attempts are exhausted in non-strict mode
IdempotencyInterceptor > fails strict mode when durable reservation is refused and releases the lock
IdempotencyInterceptor > persists 4xx HTTP exception responses for replay
IdempotencyInterceptor > persists 4xx HTTP exception responses to the durable store
IdempotencyInterceptor > does not persist 5xx HTTP exception responses (allows retry)
IdempotencyInterceptor > clears durable reservations for normal 5xx responses and non-HTTP errors
IdempotencyInterceptor > releases the lock when the handler throws synchronously
IdempotencyInterceptor > does not release a backend lock that was never acquired after a handler failure
IdempotencyInterceptor > clears durable reservations after a handler failure without a backend
IdempotencyInterceptor > replays cached responses from the durable store after a backend miss
PgIdempotencyStore > validates configured SQL identifiers
PgIdempotencyStore > returns null or false for tenantless contexts without querying
PgIdempotencyStore > maps lookup rows for pending, completed, empty, and array result shapes
PgIdempotencyStore > reserves, persists, and clears rows with expected parameters
PgIdempotencyStore > persists defined bodies and maps alternate executor result shapes
DatabaseIdempotencyStore > returns null/false/noop without a configured database
DatabaseIdempotencyStore > maps lookup rows and fallback expiry values inside system transactions
DatabaseIdempotencyStore > maps null durable row fields and default expiry fallbacks
DatabaseIdempotencyStore > reserves, persists bigint-safe responses, and clears reservations
DatabaseIdempotencyStore > returns false for skipped durable writes and uses null tenant parameters
RedisIdempotencyBackend > is inert without redis options
RedisIdempotencyBackend > stores entries, manages locks, and closes open redis clients
RedisIdempotencyBackend > returns null cache misses and uses the default redis prefix
